channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Backup QB Arbuckle leads Toronto past Winnipeg 41-24 for the Argonauts' 19th Grey Cup title
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Backup quarterback Nick Arbuckle led the Toronto Argonauts to their record-extending 19th Grey Cup title, throwing two second-half touchdown passes in a 41-24 victory over the Winnipeg Blue Bombers on Sunday night at B.C. Place.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chargers beat Bengals 34-27 on Dobbins' late TD after squandering 21-point lead
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — J.K. Dobbins rushed for a 29-yard touchdown with 18 seconds remaining, and the Los Angeles Chargers beat the Cincinnati Bengals 34-27 on Sunday night after squandering a 21-point third-quarter lead.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cavaliers to put perfect start on line against champion Celtics in juicy matchup, playoff rematch
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — The Cavaliers will put their perfect start on the line in the perfect place. “Boston Garden,” said Cleveland first-year coach Kenny Atkinson, who has yet to lose in his new job. “What's better than that?” As NBA games in November go, not much.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cunningham's 4th triple-double of the season lifts the Pistons to a 124-104 rout of Washington
item.description: Cade Cunningham had 21 points, 10 assists and 10 rebounds to lead the Detroit Pistons over the Washington Wizards 124-104.
item.dc:creator: Noah Trister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Josh Allen's 26-yard run seals Bills' 30-21 win over KC and ends Chiefs' bid for a perfect season
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — After watching quarterback Josh Allen bounce off two Kansas City Chiefs defenders before crossing the goal line on a 26-yard run, Bills receiver Khalil Shakir joined the rest of the packed Buffalo stadium by screaming at the top of his lungs.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: When Nick Bosa left with another oblique injury, Seahawks’ offense awakened and took down the 49ers
item.description: Nick Bosa's injury definitely hurt the 49ers in loss to Seahawks and there's concern he could miss additional time.
item.dc:creator: Jerry McDonald, The Mercury News

item.title: Raiders' Brock Bowers and other NFL players celebrate TDs with Trump-inspired dance moves
item.description: The Trump dance is becoming a trend around the NFL. Raiders tight end Brock Bowers, Titans wide receiver Calvin Ridley and Lions defensive end Za’Darius Smith followed Nick Bosa’s lead and celebrated big plays on Sunday with dance moves inspired by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 49ers fall after late TD pushes Seahawks past San Francisco 20-17
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Geno Smith scored on a 13-yard scramble with 12 seconds remaining and the Seattle Seahawks snapped a six-game losing streak against the San Francisco 49ers with a 20-17 victory on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Josh Allen, Bills hand Chiefs 1st defeat in nearly a year
item.description: Josh Allen and the Buffalo Bills have handed Patrick Mahomes and the Kansas City Chiefs its first loss since Christmas Day 2023, as there is no longer an undefeated team in the NFL.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Richardson's late TD run leads Colts to a 28-27 victory over Rodgers and the Jets
item.description: EAST RUTHERFORD, N.J. (AP) — Anthony Richardson was back as the Indianapolis Colts' starting quarterback — and played as though he intends to never leave the huddle again. The No.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump thanks Dana White, UFC fans for raucous welcome at MSG
item.description: President-elect Trump thanked UFC president Dana White and fight fans for the hospitality he received at Madison Square Garden on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-MLB star Jonathan Lucroy calls for voter ID requirement in elections
item.description: Former MLB star Jonathan Lucroy called for an identification requirement to be able to vote in elections. He had a discussion on social media about it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NFL fans torch officials after questionable call in Titans-Vikings game
item.description: NFL fans were roiled by another questionable call from a referee during a game between the Minnesota Vikings and Tennessee Titans on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: BYU suffers first loss of season to Kansas, 17-13
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU still has a great shot at the Big 12 Championship Game, but the Cougars undefeated season came to an end Saturday night against Kansas. Jalon Daniels threw for 169 yards, Dylan Neal ran for two touchdowns and Kansas upset No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jazz unable to hold fourth quarter lead in loss to Kings
item.description: SACRAMENTO (ABC4 Sports) - After scoring 60 points in a losing effort the night before, De'Aaron Fox scored 49 points to lead the Sacramento Kings to a 121-117 win over the Utah Jazz on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Mountain West closes investigation into allegations against San Jose State trans player without discipline
item.description: The Mountain West Conference has said it concluded an investigation into allegations against San Jose State volleyball player Blaire Fleming.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Roy High to play for first state title since 1981
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Roy High football team will play for its first state championship in 43 years. Roy defeated defending 5A champion Timpview on Friday, 29-19 to advance to next week's title game at Rice-Eccles Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Klay Thompson returns to cheers from some 400 Warriors employees
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Klay Thompson exited the team bus then beamed as he strolled back into Chase Center on Tuesday night, greeted by some 400 cheering Warriors employees who lined up along his path to the Dallas locker room to show their love and appreciation for the former Golden State star.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: BYU ranked 6th in College Football Playoff poll
item.description: PROVO, Utah (AB4 Sports) - It was one of the most thrilling victories in BYU history, and will go down as a true rivalry classic. But perhaps what was even more thrilling for the Cougars than their 22-21 win over Utah Saturday night, was the reception they received late Saturday night in Provo.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: 18-year-old arrested for throwing water bottle at football game between U of U, BYU
item.description: An 18-year-old was arrested after reportedly throwing a water bottle onto the field during the BYU-Utah game on Nov. 9.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago Sky introduce new head coach Tyler Marsh
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — A little more than a month after former head coach Teresa Weatherspoon was unceremoniously relieved of her duties, the Chicago Sky introduced their new head coach to the Windy City Tuesday. Tyler Marsh, who was hired on Nov.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Utes mired in longest losing streak in 11 years
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It has been one of the most disappointing seasons in Utah football history, and head coach Kyle Whittingham is feeling the pain. "It's been one of the most frustrating seasons of my career," Whittingham said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Gerry Faust, former head football coach at Notre Dame, has died at 89
item.description: Gerry Faust, the gravel-voiced Cincinnati high school coach who lived a dream by becoming the coach at Notre Dame, has died. He was 89.
item.dc:creator: RUSTY MILLER AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Column: Coaching cannibalism of Chicago quarterbacks continues
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The dysfunction of the Chicago Bears coaching staff is plain for all to see, and it will only continue to hamper the development of the most talented and famous football player ever drafted by the franchise if left to continue as-is.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
